2603.18856.title: Motion-o: Trajectory-Grounded Video Reasoning
2603.18856.paper_summary: Recent research has made substantial progress on video reasoning, with many models leveraging spatio-temporal evidence chains to strengthen their inference capabilities. At the same time, a growing set of datasets and benchmarks now provides structured annotations designed to support and evaluate such reasoning. However, little attention has been paid to reasoning about \emph{how} objects move between observations: no prior work has articulated the motion patterns by connecting successive observations, leaving trajectory understanding implicit and difficult to verify. We formalize this missing capability as Spatial-Temporal-Trajectory (STT) reasoning and introduce \textbf{Motion-o}, a motion-centric video understanding extension to visual language models that makes trajectories explicit and verifiable. To enable motion reasoning, we also introduce a trajectory-grounding dataset artifact that expands sparse keyframe supervision via augmentation to yield denser bounding box tracks and a stronger trajectory-level training signal. Finally, we introduce Motion Chain of Thought (MCoT), a structured reasoning pathway that makes object trajectories through discrete \texttt{<motion/>} tag summarizing per-object direction, speed, and scale (of velocity) change to explicitly connect grounded observations into trajectories. To train Motion-o, we design a reward function that compels the model to reason directly over visual evidence, all while requiring no architectural modifications. Empirical results demonstrate that Motion-o improves spatial-temporal grounding and trajectory prediction while remaining fully compatible with existing frameworks, establishing motion reasoning as a critical extension for evidence-based video understanding. Code is available at https://github.com/ostadabbas/Motion-o.
2603.18856.paper_summary_zh: 最近的研究在視頻推理方面取得了顯著進展，許多模型利用時空證據鏈來加強其推理能力。<lb/>同時，越來越多的數據集和基準現在提供了結構化的註釋，旨在支持和評估這種推理。<lb/>然而，對於物體在觀察之間如何移動的推理卻鮮有關注：之前的工作沒有通過連接連續觀察來闡明運動模式，這使得軌跡理解變得隱含且難以驗證。<lb/>我們將這一缺失的能力形式化為空間-時間-軌跡（STT）推理，並引入\textbf{Motion-o}，這是一個以運動為中心的視頻理解擴展，旨在使軌跡變得明確且可驗證。<lb/>為了實現運動推理，我們還引入了一個軌跡定位數據集工件，通過增強擴展稀疏的關鍵幀監督，以產生更密集的邊界框軌跡和更強的軌跡級訓練信號。<lb/>最後，我們介紹了運動思維鏈（MCoT），這是一個結構化的推理路徑，通過離散的\texttt{<motion/>}標籤總結每個物體的方向、速度和速度變化的尺度，明確地將定位觀察連接成軌跡。<lb/>為了訓練Motion-o，我們設計了一個獎勵函數，迫使模型直接對視覺證據進行推理，同時不需要任何架構修改。<lb/>實證結果表明，Motion-o改善了空間-時間定位和軌跡預測，同時與現有框架完全兼容，確立了運動推理作為基於證據的視頻理解的一個關鍵擴展。<lb/>代碼可在https://github.com/ostadabbas/Motion-o獲得。
2603.18856.author: Bishoy Galoaa et.al.
2603.18856.authors: Bishoy Galoaa, Shayda Moezzi, Xiangyu Bai, Sarah Ostadabbas
